title: Wave-1 plan authored and committed
description:
docs/PLAN-wave1-flywheel.md defines wave 1: CI gate lattice, labboard live
eval/test UI, shareable simlab page, Databricks readiness. Contracts in plan
S3 are NORMATIVE (NDJSON event stream, run-record JSON, gate order, labboard
HTTP surface, simlab data export). Committed in 64c0a8d. Close evidence: that
commit. All wave beads trace to this plan; agents read it at
docs/PLAN-wave1-flywheel.md in their worktree.

title: ci/gate.sh: single gate entrypoint, human + --robot NDJSON
description:
WHY: one implementation of quality semantics consumed identically by CI,
labboard, and humans (plan D1). WHAT: bash (set -euo pipefail), gates in fixed
order per plan S3.3: pytest -> determinism (simulator twice, seed 42, temp
dirs, byte-compare parquet) -> dbt build (add --vars '{enriched: true}' iff
data/enriched/title_moods.parquet exists) -> eval_gate (skip with reason if no
predictions parquet). ALL gates run even after a failure; exit 0 iff none
failed. --robot emits NDJSON per plan S3.1 (run_start/gate_start/log/gate_end/
run_end, ISO-8601 UTC ts, unbuffered). Always writes run record per S3.2 to
data/labboard/runs/<run_id>.json with trigger from --trigger flag (cli
default). --gate <id> runs one gate. Use repo venv binaries (.venv/bin).
TESTS: bats not available; add ci/test_gate.sh (bash) asserting: --robot
output is jq-parseable line-by-line, skip semantics when parquet absent, exit
codes. ACCEPT: gate.sh exits 0 on main; jq -e parses every --robot line.
Owner agent: ci. Owns ci/**. Forbidden: labboard/**, demo/**, src/**.

title: .github workflow (self-hosted linux) + ci/setup-runner.sh
description:
WHY: green Actions on a runner the operator controls (plan D5). WHAT:
.github/workflows/ci.yml: on push/PR; runs-on [self-hosted, linux]; steps:
checkout, install uv if absent, uv venv --python 3.12 + uv pip install -e
".[dev]", bash ci/gate.sh --robot --trigger ci, always() upload
data/labboard/runs/*.json as artifact. ci/setup-runner.sh: idempotent
actions-runner install for Linux x64 -> ~/actions-runner-anime-lakehouse;
reads RUNNER_URL + RUNNER_TOKEN env (refuse to run as root; refuse if vars
missing with clear usage text); ./config.sh --unattended --labels linux;
installs systemd user service via ./svc.sh; prints status. Re-run must detect
existing install and only (re)start service. NOTE in workflow comments +
runbook: self-hosted runners on PUBLIC repos execute PR code - repo stays
private until hardened. ACCEPT: actionlint-clean YAML if actionlint present
(else careful review), setup script passes shellcheck if present, dry
structure verified. Owner agent: ci. Depends on gate.sh (invokes it).

title: docs/ci-linux-box.md: Linux box CI runbook
description:
Step-by-step for a Linux box: prereqs (git, curl, python3.12 via uv,
uv install one-liner), clone path, venv setup, run gate.sh manually first,
then GitHub runner registration (where to click in repo Settings -> Actions ->
Runners -> New self-hosted runner, then RUNNER_URL=... RUNNER_TOKEN=...
ci/setup-runner.sh), systemd service verify (systemctl --user status), badge
snippet for README, and how to watch runs from labboard over ssh -L
8377:127.0.0.1:8377. Include the PUBLIC-REPO WARNING in bold. Tone: runbook,
copy-pasteable blocks, no fluff. Owner agent: ci.

title: labboard/server.py: stdlib SSE server running the gate lattice
description:
WHY: watch evals/tests execute live (plan D2/D3). WHAT: stdlib-only python
(http.server.ThreadingHTTPServer). Surface per plan S3.4 on 127.0.0.1:8377
(flags --host/--port): GET / serves labboard/index.html (read per request in
dev mode is fine); GET /api/runs lists data/labboard/runs/*.json newest-first;
POST /api/run spawns GATE_SH (env override, default ci/gate.sh) with --robot
--trigger labboard, 409 if a run is live; GET /api/stream?run_id=X is SSE
(Content-Type text/event-stream, flush per frame): forwards each NDJSON line
as one data: frame, replays data/labboard/live/<run_id>.ndjson on connect
(reconnect-safe), closes after run_end. Pump thread: reads child stdout
line-buffered, appends to live file, fans out to subscriber queues. SIGTERM
child on shutdown. No shell=True. Log to stderr. ACCEPT: manual: two browser
tabs both stream one run; smoke test bead covers automation. Owner agent:
labboard. Owns labboard/** only. Contract S3.1/S3.2 is frozen; do NOT edit
ci/** - develop against labboard/fixtures/fake_gate.sh you write yourself.

title: labboard/index.html: instrument-panel UI, both themes
description:
Single self-contained HTML (no external assets). Dark-first instrument panel,
light theme supported (prefers-color-scheme + data-theme override, token-level
CSS custom properties). Layout: header (repo name, Run gates button - disabled
while live, wired to POST /api/run); gate lattice: one card per gate with
state idle/running/pass/fail/skip encoded as color AND icon AND label (never
color alone; statuses: pass green check, fail red cross, skip grey dash,
running amber pulse honoring prefers-reduced-motion); live log pane: mono,
autoscroll with pin-to-bottom toggle, gate_start lines emphasized; history
rail: past runs (GET /api/runs) with status chip, trigger, duration, relative
time. SSE wiring via EventSource with reconnect. Type: system-ui + mono stack;
tabular-nums on durations. Palette: page #0d0d0d/#f9f9f7, surface #1a1a19/
#fcfcfb, ink #fff/#0b0b0b, accent blue #3987e5/#2a78d6, pass #0ca30c, fail
#d03b3b, muted #898781 (validated set). ACCEPT: renders with zero console
errors against server; a full run animates gate-by-gate; both themes legible.
Owner agent: labboard.

title: labboard/test_labboard.py + fake_gate fixture
description:
pytest smoke that never runs the real lattice: labboard/fixtures/fake_gate.sh
emits a canned S3.1 NDJSON stream (2 gates, one pass one skip, ~10 log lines,
50ms sleeps) and writes a fake run record. Test: start server.py on a free
port with GATE_SH pointing at fixture (subprocess), POST /api/run, read SSE
via urllib with timeout, assert: event order (run_start, gate_start<=..., run_end),
409 on concurrent POST, run record file exists and parses, GET /api/runs
includes it. Must pass under plain `python -m pytest -q` from repo root (add
to root conftest collection - tests/ discovers labboard/ via testpaths update
in pyproject [tool.pytest.ini_options] testpaths add "labboard"). Kill server
in finally. ACCEPT: green in repo venv, <15s. Owner agent: labboard.

title: scripts/export_demo_data.py: warehouse -> demo/data.json (+ --inject)
description:
Queries transform/warehouse.duckdb (read_only) and writes demo/data.json per
plan S3.5 exactly: generated_at, catalog_n, users_n, events_n, dbt_tests
{passed,total} (parse transform/target/run_results.json if present else
16/16 constant with comment), pytest {passed,total} (constant 5/5 ok, note),
retention_hist (0.025 bins), genre_means (primary_genre n>=5 only), variance_
explained {genre_eta2, tone_r2} COMPUTED (eta2 via group means; tone R2 via
recomputing _title_tone vectors from src/anime_lakehouse/ingest/
simulate_playback.py import + least squares - port the gram-solve from the
session analysis), device_qoe, titles_sample (top 15 + bottom 15 by
ep2_retention, [title, primary_genre, ep2_retention, ep1_viewers]). --inject
mode: replace contents of <script type="application/json" id="data">...</
script> in demo/simlab.html in place (regex on the id, fail loudly if block
missing). Deterministic output (sort keys, round floats 4dp). ACCEPT: runs
clean on repo warehouse; json round-trips; --inject idempotent (second run
no-op diff). Owner agent: simlab. Owns demo/** + scripts/export_demo_data.py.

title: demo/simlab.html: shareable interactive simulation page
description:
Self-contained single file (STRICT: zero network requests - inline everything;
it will be published as a claude.ai artifact AND attached to a job
application). Both themes, token CSS, system-ui + mono. Sections per plan
S4/WP-C aml-9: (1) hero thesis 'Genre explains 2.2% of episode-2 drop-off.
Latent mood explains 51%.' + two-bar variance chart (muted bar for genre null,
blue for mood); (2) retention histogram + flat genre-means bars side by side
(real data from the embedded JSON block id=data - never hand-type numbers);
(3) device QoE mini multiples: rebuffers/session and completion by device -
headline 'mobile rebuffers 2.5x, completion identical: it is not the
network'; (4) THE SIMULATOR: sliders tone-weight 0..1 (default 0.85), genre-
weight 0..0.3 (default 0.08), users 500..5000 (default 2000), seeded
mulberry32 PRNG, JS port of continuation rule p=clamp(0.15+tw*toneMatch+
gw*genreMatch, .05,.95) over synthetic titles/users derived from embedded
catalog stats; live redraw (<100ms at 2k users) of retention histogram +
recomputed variance-explained bars; 'reset to repo defaults' chip; visible
note that this is a simplified didactic port; (5) pipeline diagram (bronze->
silver->gold->enrichment->eval gate, CSS boxes) + proof strip (200 real
titles, 5,000 users, 149,223 events, 16/16 dbt, 5/5 pytest, seed-
deterministic); (6) honesty footer: events simulated with planted signal,
catalog real (AniList), same seed same bytes. Charts: inline SVG via JS,
hover tooltips per mark, direct labels, no legend for single series,
overflow-x containers, reduced-motion respected. Palette (validated): blue
#2a78d6/#3987e5, aqua #1baf7a/#199e70, red #e34948/#e66767, chrome per
labboard-ui bead. Title element: 'anime-lakehouse - simulation lab'. ACCEPT:
opens file:// zero console errors; sliders redraw live; both themes; no
external fetches (verify network tab empty). Owner agent: simlab.

title: Publish simlab as artifact (integrator only)
description:
INTEGRATOR BEAD - main session only (agents cannot publish artifacts). After
integration: re-run scripts/export_demo_data.py --inject on the merged
warehouse, open-check demo/simlab.html, publish via Artifact tool (favicon
stays consistent for the project), verify hover + themes + sliders on the
published page, paste URL into close reason and README link slot.

title: databricks bundle validate passes (or blocker documented)
description:
Install databricks CLI locally if feasible (brew install databricks or
official install script into ~/.local/bin - no sudo). Run `databricks bundle
validate` in databricks/. Fix databricks.yml schema issues (bundle target
syntax, dbt_task project_directory relative path validity, environments spec
for serverless python task, variable interpolation). Do NOT invent workspace
values - validation must pass with host unset default or documented dummy.
If validation fundamentally requires auth, capture exact command output and
document the earliest offline-checkable state honestly in the bead close +
docs. ACCEPT: either `bundle validate` output clean (paste in close reason)
or precise blocker documented in docs/databricks-free-edition.md. Owner
agent: ci. Owns databricks/** for this wave.

title: docs/databricks-free-edition.md: 30-minute deploy runbook
description:
Runbook the operator follows the evening he creates the workspace: Free Edition
signup URL + what to pick, databricks auth login OAuth flow, bundle deploy -t
dev, bundle run catalog_refresh, uv pip install -e ".[databricks]" then cd
transform && dbt build --target databricks (env vars DATABRICKS_HOST/
HTTP_PATH: where to find http_path in the workspace UI), Unity Catalog
three-part naming sanity checks (anime_lakehouse.bronze.anilist_media SELECT
count), which screenshots to take for README (bundle run green, catalog
explorer tree, dbt build summary), teardown/cost notes (Free Edition = free,
serverless only). Cross-link from README Deploy section. Owner agent: ci.

title: Integrate worktrees; run all gates independently
description:
INTEGRATOR BEAD - main session. Merge agent branches (registry-conflict rule:
only integrator touches shared files: pyproject testpaths, README, .gitignore
data/labboard). Stash any WIP first. Then INDEPENDENTLY: bash ci/gate.sh
(expect exit 0), python -m pytest -q (includes labboard smoke), export+inject
simlab data fresh, spot-run labboard server + one live run in browser. Never
trust agent-reported numbers (playbook: distrust-green). Delete worktrees +
branches after merge. Evidence in close reason: gate.sh exit + duration,
pytest counts.

title: README wave-1 update + final commit + br sync
description:
INTEGRATOR BEAD. README: add labboard section (screenshot placeholder + run
instructions), CI section (badge slot + ci-linux-box link), simlab link
(artifact URL from publish bead), Databricks runbook link. Keep de-slopified
tone: no hype adjectives, no emoji headers. Commit all wave work, br sync
--flush-only, git add .beads, commit. Close with commit hashes.

title: BACKLOG: golden-set labeling page in labboard
description:
50-title labeling helper per evals/README.md protocol: labboard route /label
serving popularity-top-50 titles (synopsis only, tags checkboxes, 1-3
enforced), writes evals/golden/title_moods_golden.jsonl, supports second-pass
shuffle + disagreement report (human ceiling metric). Unblocks real eval runs.

title: BACKLOG: live enrichment + eval with real key
description:
Needs ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (+ optional Langfuse pair). make enrich (claude-haiku-
4-5 default), make warehouse-enriched, make eval; expect gate pass vs golden
set once labeled (depends golden-labeling-helper). Record macro-F1/cost in
README results table and simlab proof strip.

title: BACKLOG: Free Edition workspace + first deploy
description:
Follow docs/databricks-free-edition.md end to end; capture screenshots for
README; then dbt build --target databricks and paste run summary. This is the
'ran it on Databricks' verification checkbox.
